---
title: "Problem Set 1: Heteroskedasticity, Clustering, and Omitted-Variable Bias"
subtitle: "EC421"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    self-contained: true
    theme:
    - cosmo
editor:
  render-on-save: true
---


## Setup

### Problem 00

Done!

### Problem 01

I'm going to load the `pacman` package and then will use it to load the `tidyverse`, `scales`, `fixest`, and `here` packages. Then, I'll read in the data from the `data-ps1.csv` file using the `read_csv()` function from the `readr` package, which is part of the `tidyverse`. The data will be stored in a data frame called `life_df`.

```{r, answer01}
# Load packages using 'pacman'
library(pacman)
p_load(tidyverse, scales, fixest, here)
# Load data
life_df = here('problem-sets', '001', 'data-ps1.csv') |> read_csv()
```

## Get to Know Your Data

### Problem 02

Now you keep going...

```{r, answer02}
# Put your code for Problem 02 here
```
